executive_summary: The Attorney-General's Department (AGD) is a policy and legal-services department (with a self-funded legal arm in the Australian Government Solicitor) responsible for Australia's law, justice, security and integrity frameworks. For 2025-26 it is leading the Commonwealth's integrity agenda (an APS Integrity Strategy, fraud control, Public Interest Disclosure, privacy reforms), driving First Nations justice and Closing the Gap reforms, responding to the family and domestic violence crisis through the National Access to Justice Partnership 2025-30, and grappling with the policy and operational implications of AI. The department openly signals capability pressure on ICT sustainability, cyber uplift, data maturity, risk-function maturity and workforce conversion under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework. As a policy/regulatory department it offers strongest BD fit in Strategy, Policy & Governance; Audit & Assurance; Data, Analytics & Information; Digital & ICT Delivery; and Programs, Projects & Change — sized proportionately, since the Strategic Commissioning Framework is actively reducing outsourcing of core ICT work.

strategic_priorities.title: Lead the Commonwealth integrity agenda
strategic_priorities.description: Develop an Integrity Strategy for the Commonwealth Public Service, steward the Commonwealth Fraud and Corruption Control Framework, Public Interest Disclosure legislation and government transparency measures, and build counter-fraud capability across government via the Commonwealth Fraud Prevention Centre.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We are also focused on our role leading the Australian Government's integrity agenda, including developing an Integrity Strategy for the Commonwealth Public Service and progressing privacy reforms.

strategic_priorities.title: Privacy reform
strategic_priorities.description: Advise on and progress reforms to the Privacy Act and coordinate government policy on data access, protection and sharing.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: playing a central role in reforms to the Privacy Act and coordination of government policy on data access, protection, and sharing.

strategic_priorities.title: First Nations justice and Closing the Gap
strategic_priorities.description: Progress First Nations Justice policy and work under the National Agreement on Closing the Gap, embedding the Four Priority Reforms, including Justice Reinvestment and trauma-informed services.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: progress work under the National Agreement on Closing the Gap, including embedding the Four Priority Reforms.

strategic_priorities.title: Family and domestic violence and access to justice
strategic_priorities.description: Respond to the FDV national crisis and deliver the National Access to Justice Partnership 2025-30 with states and territories, funding Legal Aid Commissions, ATSILS and Community Legal Centres.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Family and domestic violence remains a national crisis in Australia, placing significant demand on our courts, and legal and support services.

strategic_priorities.title: Responsible adoption of AI
strategic_priorities.description: Embrace AI to streamline processes and enhance decision-making and policy development, while advising government on AI regulation, transparency and safeguarding against misuse.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: This includes embracing artificial intelligence and other technologies to streamline processes, and enhance decision-making and policy development.

strategic_priorities.title: Criminal law fit-for-purpose and Pacific partnerships
strategic_priorities.description: Ensure criminal law and frameworks remain fit for purpose and build law-and-justice partnerships in the Pacific and Indo-Pacific.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Our efforts to ensure our criminal law and frameworks are fit for purpose, and building our partnerships in the Pacific continue to be central to our efforts.

reform_and_change_drivers: Government integrity agenda — new APS Integrity Strategy, fraud control framework stewardship, PID legislation, NACC environment
Privacy Act reforms and whole-of-government data access/protection/sharing policy
National Access to Justice Partnership 2025-30 — new five-year intergovernmental funding agreement
National Agreement on Closing the Gap and the Four Priority Reforms
Rise of AI driving both policy/regulatory demand and internal process change
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework — mandated reduction of outsourcing of core work, especially ICT & Digital Solutions ($2.19m reduction target)
New regulatory performance reporting obligations under the Regulator Performance RMG
ICT sustainability and rising technology costs; cloud adoption and cyber uplift agenda
Heightened national security threat environment and online extremist content affecting social cohesion
challenges_and_risks: Strategic risk that policy solutions are ineffective or legal/policy advice is flawed
Risk of failing to protect people, information and assets in Australia and overseas (security/cyber)
Risk of failing to comply with law and integrity frameworks
Explicit risk of failing to maintain financial sustainability
Workforce risk — attracting, recruiting and retaining people with the right skills, including digital, data and HR capability
ICT environment sustainability pressure given increasing technology costs
Risk function still maturing — developing a control effectiveness framework and emerging-risk processes
Demand pressure on courts and legal assistance from FDV crisis and cost-of-living-driven family law disputes
Tension between reducing outsourcing (Strategic Commissioning) and capability gaps, constraining external spend on core ICT

opportunity_areas.area: Commonwealth Integrity Strategy and fraud-control uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD must develop an APS-wide Integrity Strategy and continue building counter-fraud capability across government under the Fraud and Corruption Control Framework.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A governance and policy design engagement to support development of the Commonwealth Integrity Strategy framework and counter-fraud maturity model — facilitation, evidence synthesis and an implementation roadmap (with Audit & Assurance as a secondary line for control-effectiveness assessment).
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names the Integrity Strategy as a flagship 2025-26 deliverable and tasks AGD with building counter-fraud capability across government (Measure 4.3). This is enabling/policy stewardship work where independent design support is well suited.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD is central to Privacy Act reforms and coordinating whole-of-government data access, protection and sharing policy.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Policy implementation and impact-assessment support for the privacy reform program — regulatory impact analysis, stakeholder consultation design and an implementation/transition plan for affected entities.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Privacy reform is explicitly listed as a Secretary-level priority and a named whole-of-government responsibility. Multi-year reforms generate sustained advisory demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Risk-function maturity and control effectiveness framework
opportunity_areas.agency_need: The department is maturing its risk function and is developing a control effectiveness framework and emerging-risk processes.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short advisory to design and pilot a control-effectiveness framework and an enterprise/emerging-risk uplift, aligned to the Commonwealth Risk Management Policy, supporting the Chief Risk Officer and the Security and Risk Management Committee.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states AGD 'is developing a control effectiveness framework and continuing to explore options for productively surfacing and managing emerging risks' — a directly stated capability gap.

opportunity_areas.area: ICT sustainability, cloud adoption and cyber uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD admits ICT cost-sustainability pressure and commits to accelerating cloud adoption and uplifting cyber-security under its ICT Strategy 2023-2026.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment and cyber-resilience uplift roadmap covering cloud migration risks and protection of departmental systems and information (with Digital & ICT Delivery as a secondary line for cloud transition support).
opportunity_areas.rationale: The capability section explicitly lists continuing to uplift cyber-security and accelerating cloud adoption, plus ICT environment sustainability as named priorities.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD is implementing its Data Strategy 2025-2027 to make data central to decision-making and evidence-based policy.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A data-capability and business-intelligence uplift — operationalising the Data Strategy with data governance, analytics use-cases and management-reporting dashboards to support evidence-based policy advice.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan repeatedly cites the Data Strategy 2025-2027 and a commitment to making data central to decision-making at all levels, including managing strategic risk via data.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD wants to responsibly adopt AI to streamline processes and enhance decision-making and policy development.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An AI discovery and use-case prioritisation engagement — service design and process analysis to identify safe, high-value AI applications with appropriate governance guardrails.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Both the Secretary's statement and the IT systems section name responsible AI adoption as a multi-year activity. Note Strategic Commissioning may favour APS build, so scope as advisory/discovery.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD runs the Document Verification Service and Face Verification Service against strict availability (99%) and speed targets via a managed service provider.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Service performance assurance and process analysis of the DVS/FVS managed-service arrangements, or integration/transition support if the service is re-platformed.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Measures 4.7.1-4.7.2 set demanding availability and latency targets on these national identity services, indicating ongoing operational and assurance demand.

opportunity_areas.area: National Access to Justice Partnership delivery and evaluation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD administers the new NAJP 2025-30 and a range of legal financial assistance schemes with timeliness and effectiveness targets.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program management and benefits/performance support for the NAJP rollout, or an evaluation of program effectiveness against the National Strategic Framework (Strategy, Policy & Governance as secondary for evaluation design).
opportunity_areas.rationale: The NAJP 2025-30 is a new five-year partnership (Measure 4.1) with grant administration and intergovernmental reporting obligations — a multi-year delivery and evaluation footprint.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: AGD continually revises its measures (many shifted from index points to percentages; new training-satisfaction measure introduced) and its performance statements are ANAO-audited.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure review to strengthen effectiveness/efficiency measures beyond satisfaction proxies, improving auditability of the annual performance statements.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan shows heavy reliance on stakeholder-satisfaction proxies and notes ANAO audits the performance statements; this signals a measure-design improvement need.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the Chief Risk Officer (Chief People and Strategy Officer) and the Enabling Services Group with a tailored proposal on the control-effectiveness framework and emerging-risk uplift.
recommended_actions.rationale: This is an explicitly stated, in-progress capability gap with a named owner and governance forum (SRMC, ARC) — the cleanest, lowest-political-sensitivity entry point for the year.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a capability statement on integrity-framework and counter-fraud strategy design, and request a meeting with the Integrity and Security Group / Commonwealth Fraud Prevention Centre.
recommended_actions.rationale: The Integrity Strategy for the Commonwealth Public Service is a flagship 2025-26 deliverable with whole-of-government reach, creating sizeable advisory demand.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short thought piece on cloud-migration security and ICT cost-sustainability for Commonwealth policy departments, and target the ICT Committee / Enabling Services Group.
recommended_actions.rationale: AGD names cyber uplift, cloud acceleration and ICT sustainability as priorities, but the Strategic Commissioning Framework restricts core ICT outsourcing — so lead with advisory/assurance, not build.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor for procurement signals around the NAJP 2025-30 administration, privacy reform implementation and any DVS/FVS re-platforming, and engage the relevant program branches early.
recommended_actions.rationale: These are named, time-bound or multi-year initiatives likely to generate program, evaluation or delivery procurements over the plan period.

recommended_actions.action: Note and respect the Strategic Commissioning Framework when scoping any ICT/digital pitch; frame offerings as discovery, assurance or specialist advisory rather than ongoing labour hire.
recommended_actions.rationale: AGD has an explicit $2.19m target to reduce outsourcing of core ICT & Digital work — mis-pitched delivery work will be rejected.

engagement_targets: Chief Risk Officer (Chief People and Strategy Officer) — risk maturity and control-effectiveness work
Integrity and Security Group / Commonwealth Fraud Prevention Centre — integrity strategy and counter-fraud
Enabling Services Group (incl. CFO, ICT) — ICT sustainability, cloud, cyber, financial sustainability
ICT Committee and Data Governance Committee — data strategy and AI adoption
Justice and Communities Group — NAJP, legal assistance, First Nations justice, FDV programs
Audit and Risk Committee — independent assurance and governance support
Privacy/Information Law policy area — privacy reform implementation
talking_points: Your plan flags a control-effectiveness framework and emerging-risk processes as work in progress — we can help the CRO design and pilot these in a short, focused engagement.
The Commonwealth Integrity Strategy is a whole-of-government deliverable; independent facilitation and an implementation roadmap can accelerate it without straining internal teams.
We understand the Strategic Commissioning Framework — our ICT and cyber offers are scoped as discovery, security assessment and assurance, not core labour you're converting to APS.

The NAJP 2025-30 is a new five-year partnership with intergovernmental reporting obligations — we can support delivery management or an effectiveness evaluation against the National Strategic Framework.
Your performance measures lean heavily on satisfaction proxies that the ANAO audits — a PGPA-aligned measure review could strengthen auditability and tell a sharper effectiveness story.
Operationalising your Data Strategy 2025-2027 into analytics and management reporting can make data genuinely central to policy advice, as the plan intends.
caveats: This analysis is grounded solely in the corporate plan; no external research could be verified — the supplied research returned US (not Australian) results and then hit a rate limit, so news, ANAO and parliamentary sections are empty by design rather than because nothing exists. As a policy/legal-services department actively reducing outsourcing of core ICT work under the Strategic Commissioning Framework, AGD's external-spend appetite for delivery/labour may be constrained; opportunities are therefore weighted toward advisory, assurance, governance and specialist design work. Several opportunities are inferred from capability and risk statements rather than named programs, hence Medium/Low confidence. Re-running disambiguated research on news, ANAO audits and Senate Estimates is strongly recommended before any approach.
